entry.title: Platform user for Destination WM_CF_SPACE_PROVIDER in Build Process Automation
entry.content:
I configured the destination WM_CF_SPACE_PROVIDER according to the documentation https://help.sap.com/docs/build-process-automation/sap-build-process-automation/configure-destination-for-live-process-projects 

When I entered my Universal ID mail address and password, it was working. The Check Connection shows: Connection to "WM_CF_SPACE_PROVIDER" established. Response returned: "404: Not Found", so no 401 anymore.

The documentation says, I should use a platform user from the default identity provider and that I should not use a SAP Universal ID user. However the only way I got this destination working was to enter my Universal ID information.

As this destination is shared in the whole subaccount I would like to create a different user and add this to this destination (That's also what the BTP cockpit advices me to do).

Where should I create such a user? The only way I can think of is creating it as a Universal ID user, however  the documentation tells me I should not use a Universal ID user. Also, this requires me to create another mail address to keep the user separate from my Universal ID which is not nice.

entry.title: Firebase and last 4.12.107 Runtime Issues
entry.content:
Hello everyone,
I'm encountering an issue with the latest version of the Client Runtime.
My app compiles and uploads correctly to the Google Play Console, but when I download it and try to run it on my smartphone, it doesn't launch, and the screen displays this error:

 

com.facebook.react.common.JavascriptException: Error: No Firebase App '[DEFAULT]' has been created - call firebase.initializeApp(), js engine: hermes, stack:

getApp@555238:21
firebaseModuleWithApp@556316:66
anonymous@837:35
loadModuleImplementation@158:13
guardedLoadModule@96:37
metroRequire@38:91
anonymous@825:13
loadModuleImplementation@158:13
guardedLoadModule@89:46
metroRequire@38:91
global@660636:3

at com.facebook.react.modules.core.ExceptionsManagerModule.reportException (ExceptionsManagerModule.java:65)

at java.lang.reflect.Method.invoke(Native Method)

at com.facebook.react.bridge.JavaMethodWrapper.invoke(JavaMethodWrapper.java:372)

 

It seems to be an issue related to Firebase configuration but i never configured Firebase on this app.

With this version of the Client Runtime, it's the first time I've encountered this issue.
Has anyone else experienced this?

THANX!

entry.title: Commitment Item can not be assigned to G/L account although FSG is maintained (Error FIPOS 019)
entry.content:
Dear experts,

I activated the project cash management, using transaction OPI6 in SAP S/4HANA .

When I try to execute FIPOS to create commitment items, the following error appears - even after changing the FSG as mentioned in the procedure below (with T-Code OBC4). Can anyone help out? 

Many thanks in advance!

 

Errors occurred as the G/L accounts were being saved

Message no. FIPOS019

Diagnosis

The commitment item cannot be assigned to the G/L account because the state of the G/L account does not permit this transaction.

System Response

The G/L accounts affected are not saved. They have the status Inconsistent.

If you have created a new commitment item for the G/L accounts, the system saves that commitment item.

Procedure

Correct the G/L accounts, thereby making entry of commitment items possible.

Check the relevant G/L accounts: Change G/L Account.

It is possible that the commitment item field is hidden in the field status group for the G/L account.

entry.title: Re: How to change source HU during picking by using excep...
entry.content:
Hi 

I found some thing see if it works 

Use ''List'' to pick the required exception code, BIDF( Full Pick denial), system will either propose 2 things

1) It will propose an alternate HU or different storage bin

2) If it fails to find the the above, it will create a Physical inventory document.

However, BIDF can undoubtedly be used to pick quantity from different source HU,

 

Regards

Rashid Khan

entry.title: Inquiry Regarding Compliance-Proof Storage of Attachments in SAP FI Objects
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I have two questions regarding the compliance-proof storage of attachments added to SAP FI objects in S4 Public Cloud:

  1. Question: Where can we find proof that SAP FI documents, including their attachments, are stored in S4 Public cloud a compliance-proof (audit-proof) manner? Are there any official documents or certificates that confirm this?

  2. Question: Do the attachments follow the deletion and archiving retention periods of their respective SAP FI objects? Specifically, we are interested in how this can be configured in the public cloud, particularly within the scope items 5LE and 1KA and if the attachments follow the rules of the SAP Finance Object. 

Thank you in advance for your support and insights!

Best regards,

Mara

entry.title: Re: How to send emails from Quality Notification Action Box
entry.content:
Hi @gg8 

 

There is no standard function for sending an email from the quality notification in S/4HANA Cloud 

You may check the Action Box functionality whether sending an email using the follow-up action is possible. Besides fulfilling all the prerequisites, you should implement your own logic in an implementation of FOA_EXECUTION_FOR_TASK.

 

Best Regards

Enda

entry.title: Re: crystal viewer parameter loses value on navigation
entry.content:
This happens in a WEB app if you don't use sessions and postback:

1985571 - How to use sessions in web applications using the Crystal Reports viewer (the complete code)

See if that resolves the issue.

If it's a desktop app then likely you are using Refresh() in your code which will remove the current values.

Oh and I suggest updating to SP 36, CR for VS is only supported in the latest SP, SP 31 had issues in the viewer:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SUPPORT_CONTENT/crystalreports/3354091173.html

entry.content:
Hello experts,

Simple question please : 

In S4, the main table for financial data is ACDOCA, So how BSEG and BKPF are still updated in S4HANA ? System duplicates data from ACDOCA to these tables ? Or maybe tcodes update these tables in parallel of ACDOCA ?

I do understand that they still exist because of specific programs or legacy systems that may use these historical tables, but don't understand yet how they still be updated ?

Thank you!

Regards,

MK

entry.title: TECH DETAILS - E2E Oracle to SAP HANA Cloud migration with SAP Advanced SQL Migration & SAP HANA SDI
entry.content:
The purpose for this blog is to describe the End-to-End process you can follow to migrate an Oracle on-prem data model to SAP HANA Cloud including the data model conversion using SAP Advanced SQL Migration tool and the data migration using SAP HANA Smart Data Integration.

Notice that this blog may be longer that other usual blogs, this is due to the details it contains and also to the outputs attached for detail purposes.

If you are not familiarized with SAP Advanced Migration tool and the commands used during the process the following tutorial may help you, notice that the tutorial converts an Oracle on-prem database to SAP HANA on-prem, but the tutorial is not including the data migration part:

TUTORIAL | Convert Oracle-to-SAP HANA with Advanced SQL Migration Tool 

--------------------------------------------
BLOG CONTENT:

1. Prerequisites.
2. Configure a new SAP Advanced SQL Migration tool project.
3. Extract the data model using SAP Advanced SQL Migration tool Gateway.
       3.1 Start the SAP Advanced SQL Migration tool Gateway.
       3.2 Extract the data model.
       3.3 Check data model has been extracted properly.
4. Data model conversion to SAP HANA Cloud.
       4.1 Verify that data model has been converted properly.
5. Run converted SQL scripts to create the data model in SAP HANA Cloud.
6. Verify that data model & SQL objects have been created in SAP HANA Cloud.
7. Data migration.
8. Real time data replication.
--------------------------------------------

 

1.- PREREQUISITES

  • Download SAP Advanced SQL Migration tool from the following link and install it. Check installation guide inside the “docs” folder shipped with the zip installation file or in the official documentation:

Download SAP Advanced SQL Migration tool 

SAP Advanced SQL Migration tool Installation Guide 

  • The Oracle data model should be created. The following PS/SQL script is provided with all the DDLs to create the tables, sequences, functions, stored procedures, and packages into the Oracle system. The script also populates the tables with some data so that data migration can be tested in a later step:

APPDEMO2.sql 

To create the data model download the script and follow this process:

- Create an Oracle  user called “appdemo3” and give the permissions to create SQL objects.

- Download and execute APPDEMO2.sql script, this can be done with “sqlplus” client:

 

2.- CONFIGURE A NEW SAP ADVANCED SQL MIGRATION TOOL PROJECT

From the SAP Advanced SQL Migration Tool command line run sapdbmtk with “config” option specifying the project folder as parameter:

c://sapdbmtk 101# sapdbmtk config C:/APPDEMO3/HanaCloud

Initializing...

 In the first popup window select Oracle as source database and SAP HANA Cloud as target database and click “Save & Continue”:

In the following window fill in the project parameters:

The needed and suggested parameters to modify are:

Param ID #110 > IP address for the host (or hostname) where the migration tool Gateway runs.
                            (The gateway can run on the same host as the migration tool is running).
Param ID #111 > port where the gateway will listen (e.g., 12345).
Param ID #112 > set to 600.
Param ID #112 > set to “no”.
Param ID #120 > specify filter selection to extract objects: “in (‘APPDEMO3’)” should be set.
Param ID #200 > set to “no”.
Param ID #201 > set to the password used for every user created by the migration tool.
Param ID #220> set to “INT”.
Param ID #221 > set to 300.
Param ID #222 > set to “INT”.
Param ID #223 > set to “INT”.
Param ID #300 > set to “direct_select”
Param ID #330 > set to “nomap”.
Param ID #331 > set to “nomap”.
Param ID #332 > set to “nomap”.
Param ID #700 > set to “yes”.
Param ID #740 > tenant DB name in SAP HANA (in Hana Cloud this is always H00).
Param ID #751 > host where SAP HANA is running (this is the HC SQL endpoint without the port).
Param ID #752 > port where SAP HANA is running (this is always 443 for HC).
Param ID #753 > SAP HANA username, use DBADMIN to avoid having missing permissions.
Param ID #754 > SAP HANA user password for user specified in previous parameter.
Param ID #757 > set to ‘no’ (do not lock users created by the migration tool.

 

3.- EXTRACT THE DATA MODEL USING SAP ADVANCED SQL MIGRATION TOOL GATEWAY

3.1.- Start the SAP Advanced SQL Migration tool Gateway

Configure and start the migration tool Gateway using the host and port indicated in the project parameters #110 and #11. Details to perform the configuration can be found in the migration tool user guide or the tutorial indicated at the beginning of this blog:

3.2.- Extract the data model

The migration tool “extract” command is used to connect to the source database and extract the data model, the usage for this command is:

sapdbmtk extract <project_folder> <server_name> <server_host> <port> <db_user> <db_user_passwd>

In this case the command is:

sapdbmtk extract C:/APPDEMO3/HanaCloud XE 192.168.1.179 1521 system XXXXXXX

NOTE: two compliance popups may appear, please click on “I confirm” an execute the same "extract" command again.

Check the end of the output in the command line to see that no errors happen:

c://sapdbmtk 106# sapdbmtk extract C:/APPDEMO3/HanaCloud XE 192.168.1.179 1521 system XXXXXXX
Initializing...
 

Logfile for this session: C:/APPDEMO3/HanaCloud/reports/XE.SQL-Extraction.Oracle-HANA.2024Oct16.163632.log

SAP Advanced SQL Migration v.3.5.4, September 2024

================================================================================
>>> The installation/operation guidelines as described in the                <<<
>>> SAP Advanced SQL Migration documentation must be strictly adhered to.    <<<
>>> Copyright (c) 2024 SAP SE. All rights reserved.                          <<<
>>> Use, disclosure, reproduction or distribution of this program is         <<<
>>> prohibited except as expressly allowed by the governing license          <<<
>>> agreement or written permission of SAP SE.                               <<<
>>> Modification, reverse engineering and de-obfuscation are not permitted.  <<<
================================================================================

================================================================================

No DBMS licensing compliance confirmation record found. Confirmation of sufficient licensing rights in the source database is required to continue.

Run 'sapdbmtk' (without arguments) for commands and options.

===== End ======================================================================

c://sapdbmtk 108#

3.3.- Check data model has been extracted properly

A new subfolder called “extracted” should appear in the project folder, you should see there a file for every extracted stored procedure, function, and trigger and one subfolder called “catalog” with the internal metadata about the extracted data model (columns, indexes …etc.):

4.- DATA MODEL CONVERSION TO SAP HANA CLOUD

Once the data model is extracted, you need to convert to SAP Hana Cloud, this is done with the “convert” migration tool command:

sapdbmtk convert <project_folder>

In this case the command is:

sapdbmtk convert C:/APPDEMO3/HanaCloud

Check the end of the output in the command line to check that no errors happen:

c://sapdbmtk 109#

 4.1.- Verify that data model has been converted properly

A new subfolder called “scripts” should appear in the project folder, a set of SQL scripts plus shell scripts are generated and they should be executed to create the data model in SAP HANA Cloud:

 SQL scripts:

dbmtk_create_users.sqlscript  > SQL script to create the database users.
dbmtk_create_tables.sqlscript > SQL script to create the converted tables.
dbmtk_create_views.sqlscript  > SQL script to create the converted view.
… etc.

Shell scripts:

run_ddl_scripts.sh  > shell script to create the data model (users, tables, views, PKs, ...etc.)
run_sql_scripts.sh  > shell script to create SQL objects containing SQL code (stored procedures,functions and triggers)
run_sql_scripts_constraints.sh  > shell script to constraints and foreign keys, typically executed after the data migration

 

5.- RUN CONVERTED SQL SCRIPTS TO CREATE THE DATA MODEL IN SAP HANA CLOUD

Script run_ddl_scripts.sh should be executed first, it is a shell script so a Unix command line is required, you can use a migration tool command line (the migration tool runs on top of Cygwin Unix env for Windows):

Total files          : 42
Files w/zero messages: 0

===== end of script: run_sql_scripts.sh =====

Session log: run_sql_scripts.out

c://appdemo3/HanaCloud/scripts 103#

 

6.- VERIFY THAT DATA MODEL AND SQL OBJECTS HAVE BEEN CREATED IN SAP HANA CLOUD

Open the BTP Cockpit - Database Explorer and check that the tables and other SQL objects are created under a schema APPDEMO3:

 

7.- DATA MIGRATION

The data migration can be performed in very different ways depending on each case, any ETL tool can be used, for example SAP Data Services. Export/import to/from files in a file storage is another possibility. In this case the data migration is going to be done using SAP HANA Smart Data Integration, using this you also have the possibility to activate real time data replication as it will be shown in bullet #8.

An SAP HANA Cloud remote source pointing for the Oracle database is required both for the data migration and the real time replication. To make this work properly some configuration is required in the SAP HANA Cloud instance and also in an on-prem host with access to the Oracle database instance. In the on-prem host SAP HANA Data Provisioning Agent needs to be installed and configured, in the SAP HANA instance the remote source has to be created once the SAP HANA Data Provisioning Agent is configured.

DP Agent configuration and SAP HANA Cloud instance configuration is out of the scope of this blog, so I am not entering into the deep details, check these links and sub-links carefully and follow the process to configure them properly:

SDI Configuration Guide for SAP HANA Cloud | SAP Help Portal

The SDI adapter I am using for this configuration is the Oracle log reader using “LogMiner” as capture mode, this adapter requires some specific configuration in the Oracle side as well, for example for real time data replication supplemental logging must be enabled in the Oracle Server. Details about how to configure the adapter can be found here:

SDI Oracle Log Reader | SAP Help Portal

Once everything is configured you should be able to create a remote source pointing to the Oracle on-prem database, this is how it looks like in my demo environment (the name of the remote source is ORA11):

This remote source is accessing the real physical tables in the Oracle on-prem system:

Now you can create virtual tables pointing the Oracle systems and populate the SAP HANA Cloud tables selecting from those virtual tables, this is done with “insert … select …” statements. This is what you need to execute to get this done:

This is the output executing these commands:

And you can check that the data has been migrated to your SAP HANA Cloud database:

 

8.- REAL TIME DATA REPLICATION

For demo purposes real time replication is going to be activated only for the AUTHORS table, these are the commands you need to run for that and the output you obtain:

Now you can insert a new author in the Oracle on-prem database, you can do it with the stored procedure the application is using for that purpose (appdemo3.insert_author):

call appdemo3.insert_author ('Javier', 'Martin', '12345', 'address1', 'Madrid', 'MA', 'Spain', '11111', null);

Oracle sqlplus client can be used to execute the procedure call and to check the new author:

And finally, you can check that the new author is replicated into the SAP HANA Cloud database:

A demo video with more visual aspects about this migration can be seen here:

DEMO - E2E Oracle to SAP HANA Cloud migration with SAP Advanced SQL Migration & SAP HANA SDI 

 

I hope you have enjoyed this blog and good luck with your database migration to SAP HANA Cloud.

 

Community: SAP Advanced SQL Migration 

Contact:  Advanced_SQL_Migration@sap.com

entry.title: Re: Add Transalation button to SAP Enable Now navigation bar
entry.content:
This button is available to authors and learners by default.

If not, in the manager, just go to Administration -> Permissions and check if they have got this permission "User: Allow Machine Translation".

Importantly, this button is only visible if you play the content from manager.

entry.title: Welcome Another Great Add-on to the PLM System Integration Family
entry.content:
Dear PLM community,

A few weeks ago, I gave you an overview of the newcomers in the PLM system integration family in my blog post SAP and Selected Partners Collaborate to Provide New PLM Integration Add-ons. Today, I am very happy to announce that another great add-on is available for you: With PLM system integration, add-on for Autodesk Vault, SAP offers you a great tool for transferring your engineering data into your SAP ERP system, be it SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, SAP S/4HANA, or SAP ERP.

Use this add-on to integrate your SAP ERP system with Autodesk Vault Professional, a product data management (PDM) solution that leverages Autodesk design tools and other CAD systems to centralize data and streamline workflows across engineering, manufacturing, and beyond. Autodesk Vault Professional fosters collaboration and offers reliable versioning capabilities, for smooth and efficient data exchange.

Integration With SAP Solutions

PLM system integration, add-on for Autodesk Vault connects your Autodesk Vault Professional system to your SAP ERP system, offering you a smooth, out-of-the box integration. Whether you are using SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud or Private Cloud Edition, SAP S/4HANA or SAP ERP solutions, our integration will help you meet your business requirements with unprecedented flexibility.

Extensive Features Supporting Your Individual Business Processes

Our first release of PLM system integration, add-on for Autodesk Vault features a rich list of capabilities designed to empower engineers and product development teams.

    1. Autodesk Engineering Items to SAP Materials Transfer
      Transfer classic enterprise-level bill of materials, including date effectivity, from Autodesk Vault Professional to SAP, based on business semantics, aligning product revisions and attributes, connecting engineering and the extended supply chain.
    2. BOMs Under Version Control
      Maintain bill of materials under version control in your SAP system via the integration, enhancing master data quality with semantic information and increasing transparency across various domains.
    3. Revision Level Support
      Assign and transfer revision levels of document info records and material masters as part of a change process and create revision levels based on a document or product version entity.
    4. Leverage MDM-Driven Master Data Creation
      Enable SAP’s master data management solution in the context of the material master creation process, including the use of material master number reservation and MDG change request creation.
    5. Advanced BOM Management Integration
      Allow engineers to maintain alternative UoMs for material masters directly in the product structure, streamlining correct material master creation and improving master data quality.
    6. Document Management Integration
      Maintain document info records in your SAP system and in Autodesk Vault Professional, aligning business semantics. Create and update document info records based on Autodesk Vault Professional objects, providing 3D visual data access for all stakeholders, and sharing information between the engineering department and the extended supply chain.
    7. Cross-system Data Federation
      Show entity data of the integrated system in Autodesk Vault Professional to access business-critical data in the context of a leading object, providing a       holistic view of the material master and document info record.

Looking Ahead: Future Road Maps and Innovations

As we continue to innovate, you can look forward to more and enhanced features:

Take a closer look at what’s to come in the SAP Road Map Explorer.

All relevant information on PLM system integration is available via the links below.

 

      • PLM system integration, add-on for Autodesk Vault
      • PLM system integration for SAP S/4HANA
      • SAP S/4HANA Cloud: PLM System Integration With Non-SAP Systems
      • PLM system integration for SAP ERP
      • SAP Product Availability Matrix (SAP login required):
        • Matrix for PLM system integration 3.0 for SAP S/4HANA
        • Matrix for PLM system integration 2.0 for SAP ERP
      • SAP Roadmap Explorer:
        • SAP Roadmap for PLM system integration

And last but not least –  contact us via our excellent forum if you have any questions.

entry.title: How can I parallelize the training of a HANA_ML MLPRegressor?
entry.content:
According to the documentation setting thread_ratio=1 means all available threads on SAP HANA will be used for training, but I can only see 5 threads of the scriptserver being active. This is exactly the same behavior as when training with the default value of thread_ratio=0. How can I speed up the training via parallelization?

from hana_ml.algorithms.pal.neural_network import MLPRegressor

mlp = MLPRegressor(hidden_layer_size=(layer1,layer2),
activation='relu',
output_activation='relu',
max_iter=iterations,
weight_init='normal',
training_style='batch',
normalization='z-transform',
thread_ratio=1)

mlp.fit(train_df, features=x_columns, label='y')

entry.title: Re: How to increase the length of fields in Form Templates

You can create a Custom Form Template based on the Standard Form Template you are using and then change the field property 'Max Chars' to the one that will attend your needs (I.e. 40 chars).

Please refer to the official documentation: How to Create and Use a Custom Form Template | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: Re: Practice Mode sim and Training Document Output
entry.content:
In the producer, go to Resources -> Documentation Settings -> and select your document type.

In your right hand side, you will find "Project Content" section and also "Bubble Text From" option.

Choose Practice Bubble option. 

So whenever you generate documents, you will get the text only from practice mode bubble.

entry.title: Key Differences Between SAP Central Business Configuration and the Implementation Activities App
entry.content:
Are you struggling to understand when to use SAP Central Business Configuration and when to use the Implementation Activities app in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, 3-system landscape? No problem, let’s clear that up.

SAP Central Business Configuration is best used for configuring business processes based on your specific requirements. This involves performing configuration activities, accessing setup instructions or setup guides, and maintaining an overview of the statuses of these activities. You can also download a list of configuration activities for offline use and reference in the future.

To easily identify the right configuration activities, you can search by name or configuration activity ID. Furthermore, you can make use of the various filter options that enable you to filter by status, go-live relevance or business area. Moreover, the Country/Region filter allows you to filter configuration activities that are relevant to one or multiple countries/regions in scope or globally. This should come in handy especially for scope extensions that involve new countries/regions.

Screenshot of the Configure Your Business Processes app in SAP Central Business Configuration

The Implementation Activities app is more suited for displaying and maintaining current settings in the development, test, and production systems. It allows you to compare configuration activities among these systems and view change logs for the configuration activities.

Screenshot of the Implementation Activities app in SAP S/4HANA Cloud

To learn more, you can go to the SAP Help Portal for further details about configuration activities in SAP Central Business Configuration and the Implementation Activities app in SAP S/4HANA Cloud. To stay in the know, make sure to follow the SAP Central Business Configuration tag or follow this dedicated blog collection to stay up to date with the latest blog posts.

Do you have an idea on how to further enhance SAP Central Business Configuration? Go to the SAP Customer Influence portal to submit an improvement request or vote for the ideas of others. This helps us understand your requirements even better and prioritize our work accordingly. We’re looking forward to your improvement requests.

entry.title: Re: Generic groovy script to update json data types in json payload
entry.content:
Hi ,

Thank you for sharing the detailed explanation of how to update JSON data types using a Groovy script in SAP BTP Cloud Integration. This approach is essential for ensuring data types are correctly represented in the output JSON, especially when dealing with APIs that require specific formats.

Here’s an alternative Groovy script you might find useful:

import com.sap.gateway.ip.core.customdev.util.Message
import groovy.json.JsonSlurper
import groovy.json.JsonOutput

def Message processData(Message message) {
def payload = message.getBody(String)
def parsedJson = new JsonSlurper().parseText(payload)
def properties = message.getProperties()
def list = message.getProperty("input_list")
def map = [:]
list.split(",").each {
def (key, value) = it.trim().split(":")
map[key.trim()] = value.trim()
}

map.each { propertyName, jsonKey ->
def keys = propertyName.split(/\./)
def output = parsedJson
keys.eachWithIndex { key, index ->
if (index == keys.size() - 1) {
output[key] = convertToType(output[key], jsonKey)
} else {
output = output[key]
}
}
}

message.setBody(JsonOutput.toJson(parsedJson))
return message
}

def convertToType(value, type) {
switch (type.toLowerCase()) {
case "integer": return value.toInteger()
case "boolean": return value.toBoolean()
case "decimal": return value.toBigDecimal()
default: return value
}
}

Regards ,

entry.title: Latest SAP Utilities Headline News - October Issue Out Now!
entry.content:
Dear Utilities Community,

Welcome to a new edition packed with exciting updates!

Get ready for our upcoming Espresso Session dedicated to Customer Experience for Utilities, where you will meet “FLASH” the Enterprise Maturity Assessment tool that identifies business gaps and new growth opportunities. Also, make a note in your diary of the next session focusing on the potential of GenAI in the Utilities industry; experience innovative AI-powered scenarios, real-world cases and SAP’s AI solutions that can take your operations to the next level.

 


We are also excited to celebrate the success of our customers! Liberty Utilities has recently introduced a new customer experience platform aimed at empowering its employees with the latest tools to provide exceptional customer service. Meanwhile, Squadron Energy is driving growth by automating their operations with SAP. Enerix Westerwald-Bonn has implemented SAP solutions to streamline processes, enhance customer service, and improve efficiency. SK E&S is advancing its mission for a sustainable future by modernizing its ERP system with SAP S/4HANA Cloud private edition. ZSE Energia has streamlined its VAT processing while ensuring compliance with local tax regulations, and EdERSA is speeding up transactions and enhancing security with SAP Business Network Commerce Automation.

Our newsletter is packed with even more news and updates. You can read the complete newsletter here. 

Sign up here to get it sent directly to your inbox. 

Talk to you soon, 

Nicole

entry.title: Enterprise structure Retail Business

Would each shop have be an individual plant or storage location? The different shop's products will most likely have different cost prices and selling prices.

 

Kind Regards 

Luba

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hello there David! 🙂

Please see the screenshots of the current status of my registered SAP account :  

 

I am a BCom Accounting for Chartered Accountants graduate from Nelson Mandela University.

I'm currently registered to postgrad studies, specifically CTA/PGDA, in my pursuit of becoming a designated Chartered Accountant.

I have signed a pre-agreement to complete my articles at Ernst & Young, from January 2026. It is not binding however.

I performed a few assessments a couple years ago at Nelson Mandela University for SAP ERP South Africa. I was one of the few students who were selected to be trained, complete all required certifications, and get free exam opportunity as a result.

My trainer was Ricardo from SAP South Africa - Durban.

Due to exams, and other very serious commitments, I was not able to complete the courses and certifications in the allotted time to take up the free exam opportunity.

But I am still interested in learning and being certified in SAP ERP.

I have just completed my CTA studies for the year, so I would be very grateful and indebted if I could get a 2nd opportunity to complete the S/4HANA (public and private) courses, fulfil all the required certifications, and write the exams, now that I am free of any priorities.

I fall into the following categories :

    • People in remote or underserved locations that may be not have access to the same economic opportunities as their urban or more privileged counterparts; 
    • Minorities who may not have equal opportunity in their geographic location ;
    • First generation college students; and
    • People who lack the financial means to afford a certification attempt for themselves 

I am super desperate to learn all that I can about SAP software and to undergo the rigorous studying schedule needed to achieve SAP Certification.

I am completely clueless as to how I should go about it all.

Please may I be considered for the opportunity being provided. Also could you assist me as to what to do.

Thank you for giving me the chance to upskill myself in preparation for my career.

Tokollo L.S. Damons

tokollodamons@gmail.com

entry.title: Re: Employee Code Sequence Requirement
entry.content:
Hi @rashmiranjan1411 , hope you're doing fine.

I think that Generating User IDs with Business Rules guide may help you to start your requirement.

I believe you can get these data that will be concatenated by using some configuration available in business rules, like variables for calculation purposes, lookup function to search for values in a MDF that you will have to create with the company codes and their respective values, and number ranges creation to get the sequential you need in your final concatenated string.

Please check the Business Rule Guide to know more about above information, and then share your results in case you need any additional support.

Good luck with your requirement.
Best Regards.

Thiago Eva

entry.title: Re: DRF - Business Partner replication - disable confirma...
entry.content:
You are referring to the "MDG_KM_MAINTAIN" key mapping web service in this replication, right? We don't use it in this replication.

The number of business partners is approximately 30,000 per day. This is a change replication.

The PACK_SIZE_BULK is set to 1 because of error handling. We cannot restart the messages in srt_moni; if an error occurs for one business partner, the other business partners within the message are also not processed. It is easier to figure out which business partner has an error and resend it.
